preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.20.3: HOTFIX — Klaviyo bulk-template-upload pacing. Live incident on the POG 27-flow import: ~10 templates landed cleanly, then 8 of the next batch returned 429 from Klaviyo, marking the files as permanently failed and requiring a manual re-run. Three stacked causes: (1) klaviyoBulkUploadConcurrency was 3, three goroutines hit PreflightCheck within the same nanosecond, all observed '0 calls in last minute' before any RecordSuccess incremented — thundering herd; (2) Klaviyo's POST /api/templates/ per-minute cap is plan-dependent, free/email tiers can be ~10/min vs the documented 150/min steady the ratelimit_preflight default assumed; (3) klaviyoCreateTemplate surfaced 429 verbatim with no retry. Fix: concurrency 3 → 1 (sequential, eliminates herd race), 6s minimum spacing between calls (10/min ceiling), klaviyoCreateTemplateWithRetry wraps with a single 35s-backoff retry on 429. Trade-off: 27-template import goes from ~9s to ~3min — acceptable for a one-time setup. lastCallAt updates on failure too so a 429 doesn't fire the next call into a hot Klaviyo window. New tests: TestIsKlaviyoRateLimitError pins the 429 substring contract, TestKlaviyoCreateTemplateWithRetry_NoRetryOnSuccess verifies happy path skips the retry sleep. v1.20.2: Postscript SMS full-coverage automation API + bulk-flow import. Postscript exposes Automations (their flow analog) as a fully-scriptable public API, unlike Klaviyo Flows which are dashboard-only — so the morning POG SMS setup can land as a single mcp__merlin__postscript({action: 'bulk-import-flow', manifestPath, brand}) call where the equivalent Klaviyo path is templates-only. New action surface (12 new actions): automation-get / automation-create / automation-update / automation-delete / automation-activate / automation-deactivate / automation-steps / automation-step-create / automation-step-update / automation-step-delete / bulk-import-flow. Bulk import takes a JSON manifest under assets/brands/<brand>/sms/flows.json (refused otherwise — same path-traversal safety rail as the Klaviyo session); runs a four-rule TCPA gate on every flow BEFORE any HTTP call; refuses (never auto-fixes) flows that violate. The four rules: (1) every flow's first send_message MUST contain Reply-STOP opt-out language (literal or via {{UNSUB_REPLY}} which token-swaps to 'Reply STOP to unsubscribe'); (2) trigger.type must be on the documented-consent allowlist (subscriber_added_to_list / keyword / checkout / checkout_completed / product_purchased / cart_abandoned / browse_abandoned / back_in_stock — manual-segment triggers refused as no-consent-guarantee); (3) brand must have postscriptTenDLCID configured (US 10DLC mandatory); (4) flow must have ≥1 send_message step. Token swap rewrites generic placeholders ({{FIRST_NAME}}, {{COUPON_CODE}}, {{COUPON_URL}}, {{SHOP_URL}}, {{UNSUB_REPLY}}) to Postscript-native merge tokens before send. Live-state dedup: bulk-import lists existing automations once and refuses to create a duplicate-by-name (idempotent re-imports); pass forceReimport=true to override. Two-phase fan-out: Phase 1 sequential gate (CheckFlowTCPA + dedup, pure CPU), Phase 2 ExecuteBatch('postscript', createRequests) + ExecuteBatch('postscript', activateRequests) — both use the registered postscriptBatchExecute (ParallelFetch concurrency 4). Every outbound api.postscript.io call goes through PreflightCheck + RecordSuccess/RecordRateLimitHit per CLAUDE.md hard rule. New tests: 31 Go tests in postscript_automations_test.go (token swap × 4, TCPA gate × 7, automation CRUD × 9, step CRUD × 4, manifest path-traversal × 3, bulk-import × 4 + parallelism guard); 4 new Node tests in mcp-tools.test.js (description-surface contract + routing-prefix oracle). v1.20.1: Klaviyo email template bulk upload — drop a folder of HTML emails and Merlin uploads them all to Klaviyo with one MCP tool call (klaviyo({action: 'templates-bulk-upload', brand, dir, nameTemplate})). New action surface: templates-list / template-get / template-create / template-update / template-delete / templates-bulk-upload, all routed through klaviyo_templates.go in the Go binary so they ride the existing vault-decrypt path (the user's raw API key is AES-256-GCM-encrypted on disk; only the binary can read it — a Python script that reads klaviyoApiKey from .merlin-config-<brand>.json gets a 401 because the field is the @@VAULT:klaviyoApiKey@@ placeholder, not the raw key). Token swap is opt-in (default true for bulk-upload, false for single template-create): translates {{UNSUB_URL}} → {{ unsubscribe }}, {{ FIRST_NAME }} → {{ first_name|default:'there' }}, {{ EMAIL }} → {{ person.email }}, {{COMPANY_NAME}} / {{COMPANY_ADDRESS}} from brand.md. Bulk-upload validates the directory is inside assets/brands/<brand>/ (no arbitrary FS access), caps each HTML at 5 MB, fans out via ParallelFetch at concurrency 3 (matches klaviyoBatchExecutor's cap, well inside Klaviyo's burst budget), and returns a structured per-file pass/fail report so chat can show 'uploaded 49 of 51, 2 failed: <names>' with no confabulation. Klaviyo Flows themselves remain UI-only — flow construction (trigger, branches, time delays, message slot wiring) is not in the public API as of revision 2024-10-15, and the merlin-social SKILL surfaces this manual step verbatim after every bulk upload so the LLM never tells the user 'I created your flows.' Live incident anchor (2026-04-29, POG): Ryan tried to bulk-import 51 templates for a 27-flow / 51-email setup; the existing klaviyo MCP tool only exposed performance / lists / campaigns; falling back to a Python script hit the 401 above. 25 new Go tests + 4 new MCP tests cover token swap, pagination, oversize rejection, dir traversal rejection, brand-slug validation, partial-success counts, and applyTokens=false byte-preservation. v1.20.0: MCP-direct sidecar — use Merlin's full tool surface from Claude Desktop (or any MCP client: Codex, Cline, Cursor) instead of being tied to the in-app chat. Three additive components, zero regressions to existing flows. (1) Local IPC endpoint inside the desktop app (app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.js) — Unix domain socket on macOS/Linux (mode 0o600), Windows named pipe under default-deny ACL. Newline-delimited JSON protocol, three methods only: ping / tools/list / tools/call. NO vault/get, NO config/read — the endpoint refuses anything else, so a compromised shim cannot exfiltrate keys. Auth via 32-hex token rotated on every desktop-app boot, written to <stateDir>/mcp-shim-token (mode 0o600), constant-time-compared at every dispatch (Hard-Won Security Rule 5). The same wrapped tool array the in-app SDK uses is dispatched here — single registry, two consumers, brand-check + idempotency + preview gate + concurrency slot + redaction all apply uniformly. (2) Stdio MCP shim (app/merlin-mcp-shim.js) — ~400 lines, asar-unpacked so Claude Desktop can spawn it directly with the bundled Node binary. JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout. Active-brand auto-injection: if the call is missing brand AND the renderer's <appRoot>/.merlin-state.json has activeBrand set, the shim injects it before forwarding. Mirrors the in-app [ACTIVE_BRAND] tag flow. Graceful degradation: if the desktop app isn't running, tools/list returns a single placeholder tool whose call surfaces a friendly 'open Merlin and try again' message instead of breaking the connection. (3) Claude Desktop autoconfig (app/claude-desktop-config.js) — atomic merge of {mcpServers: {merlin: {command, args}}} into the user's claude_desktop_config.json. Preserves all other mcpServers entries (Cline, Cursor, etc.) untouched. One-time prompt at first launch with three buttons: Add now / Skip / Don't ask again. Decision persisted to <stateDir>/.mcp-claude-desktop-prompt; 'never' suppresses forever, 'skipped' re-prompts only on a major version bump. Refuses to clobber a corrupt config file. Renderer-driven 'Add to Claude Desktop' button wired via mcp-autoconfig-add IPC; sidecar-status query via mcp-autoconfig-status. SAFETY: this is purely additive — the in-app SDK chat is unchanged, the in-process MCP server still uses the same tool array, no flow that worked in v1.19.5 has changed. Boundary preservation verified: vault stays in Electron, never touches the shim or Claude Desktop process; only tool inputs and redacted outputs cross IPC. Tests: app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.test.js (24 cases) + app/merlin-mcp-shim.test.js (28 cases) + app/claude-desktop-config.test.js (19 cases). | v1.19.5: SDK parity RSI — five fixes landing together to close the felt gap with Claude.ai / Claude Desktop after Ryan called the in-app chat experience 'noticeably horrid' on 2026-04-29 (POG brand, live incidents listed below). Read alongside D:\autoCMO-claude\CLAUDE.md's Engineering Standard section. (A) Mid-turn message queue — silent-drop bug fix + visible badge. messageGenerator's queue drain at app/main.js:2691 lived BEFORE the while(true) await loop, so any message typed during a tool call (resolveNextMessage was null) landed in pendingMessageQueue and was never delivered when the SDK pulled the next message. The drain now runs INSIDE the loop, BEFORE every await — a queued message is delivered on the next turn boundary. Paired UX fix: main.js fires 'message-queued' (depth grows) and 'message-queue-drained' (depth shrinks) IPC events, the renderer's chat-status row now renders a 'queued (N)' badge fed by the pure queueBadgeReducer (app/sdk-parity.js, 9 unit tests). Live incident: Ryan typed '?' and 'helloe?' mid-turn while a tool was hung; both messages disappeared. (B) Mandatory pre-tool grounding — merlin-content/SKILL.md now opens with a five-step MANDATORY pre-tool checklist (brand resolved, product named, references loaded from products/<slug>/references/, brand.md read, pre-tool status emitted) that gates EVERY image/video/voice/refine call. Live incident: agent generated 12 product images for POG without loading references; output was generic, $1+ wasted, 36min wall clock burned. The checklist is the prevention. (C) Confabulation prevention — universal rule in commands/merlin.md: when a tool returns an error, surface it VERBATIM. Never paraphrase into a plausible-sounding root cause without supporting evidence. Banned: invented internals like 'the binary's slug registry' (incident: agent confabulated this when fal returned a plain 404). app/sdk-parity.js exports isRawErrorVerbatim helper (24-char-window heuristic) for future telemetry-side flagging. (D) Pre-tool status discipline — strengthened the rule to require ALL FOUR concrete fields (action, count, context, ETA-or-cost) for any tool >15s OR >$0.05. Vague 'Working on it…' is a regression. Concrete reference example baked in: 'Generating 12 images of POG cherry using 4 references from products/pog-cherry/references/ — ~36min, ~$1.20 fal spend.' app/sdk-parity.js exports formatPreToolStatus that returns null if any required field missing — gives the LLM a programmatic STOP signal. (E) Streaming responsiveness audit — main.js startSession's for-await loop and renderer's textBuffer flush were inspected. accountInfo() is already fire-and-forget (REGRESSION GUARD 2026-04-27); no new sync I/O introduced in v1.19.5. The remaining chunky-feel vs claude.ai is structural — Anthropic SDK's streaming cadence + the SDK subprocess boundary — and not addressable without rewriting the entire SDK invocation. Flagged in PR body, not pretended-fixed. Tests: app/sdk-parity.test.js (26 tests covering reducer state machine, pre-tool status formatter rejection rules, raw-error verbatim heuristic, queue badge copy, pluralizer); existing test suites (oauth-persist 15, bulk-upload 26, captions, paste-drop 28, validate-skills 10) green; routing-corpus extended with 4 new merlin-content utterances covering 'make 12 ads of the cherry product' / 'shoot ad images using the photos I uploaded' / etc. v1.19.4: Three hardenings landing together to prevent the 2026-04-29 trypog.co WebFetch hang from recurring + clean up the misleading Meta-token error chain. (1) Brand-URL routing rule — commands/merlin.md now hard-pushes 'go to my site' / 'rescrape <url>' / 'check products on <url>' intents to mcp__merlin__brand_scrape (which has the 5/15/90s layered timeouts from CLAUDE.md Rule 13). The thin always-loaded router teaches the LLM to never WebFetch the user's own brand URL — built-in WebFetch has no enforced timeout and hung for 5+ minutes on a slow CDN. Universal rule added: WebFetch is a last-resort tool, used only when no MCP tool covers the intent; pre-fetch tells the user 'this can hang, hit Cancel if it stalls.' (2) Cancel button on the chat-status row — fades in at 30s of stuck status, calls the new abort-active-query IPC which interrupt()s the active SDK turn, drains the pending message queue, clears pending tool approvals, and broadcasts query-aborted so the renderer can clear the spinner + show a 'Cancelled' toast. Re-uses the same activeQuery.interrupt() machinery the brand-switch handler has used since v1.18.x. Means future hung tools are recoverable in one click instead of force-quitting Merlin. (3) Meta empty-token error message hardening — five fatal() sites in meta.go (2331, 2841, 3587, 3782, 4033) all said 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again,' which is exactly what the user had JUST done via the right-click 'Use my API key' modal in the trust-loss incident. New tokenMissingDiag helper (token_required.go) builds context-aware messages: brand-context → 'token saved correctly but invisible to this code path, restart Merlin and retry'; no-brand-context → 'connect Meta via the Magic panel tile, or paste a long-lived token via right-click "Use my API key".' 4 unit tests pin both cases and assert the pre-fix misleading hint can never reappear. Audit list for extending to tiktok/google/linkedin/amazon kept inline in token_required.go for follow-up. v1.19.3: HOTFIX — Meta manual-token modal: pasting an access token via right-click → 'Use my API key' on a brand-scoped tile (e.g. POG) silently failed with 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again', even though the token was correctly persisted to the brand vault. Root cause: ipcMain.handle('discover-meta-ids') in app/main.js spawned the Go binary with --config <global-config> only — the binary's loadConfig does NOT auto-merge .merlin-config-<brand>.json from disk, so the @@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@ placeholder (which the renderer wrote to the brand config file) never made it into the binary's Config struct, applyBrandVault had nothing to resolve, and cfg.MetaAccessToken stayed empty. Dashboard refresh works because it builds a strict brand-merged config in a temp file via buildStrictBrandConfig and passes THAT as --config (line ~6253). discoverMetaIds now mirrors the dashboard pattern exactly — temp config, brand-merged, deleted on completion (no decrypted credentials left on disk). REGRESSION GUARD comment added at the fix site documenting the live incident (brand=pog, 2026-04-28, screenshot in chat). v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. The IPC backend stays shipped, refactored into a reusable helper that's exposed to the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense. | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: Fixed: Klaviyo bulk-template-upload now reliably finishes large batches (27+ templates) without rate-limit failures. Sequential pacing + 6s spacing + automatic retry on 429 means the import takes ~3 min instead of ~9s, but actually completes — versus the prior path where ~10 templates would land then ~8 would 429 and get marked permanently failed.

Postscript SMS flow import — drop a JSON manifest of automations (triggers + steps + messages) under assets/brands/<brand>/sms/ and Merlin creates them all via API. Unlike Klaviyo Flows (UI-only), Postscript Automations are fully scriptable end-to-end, so the morning SMS setup goes from 30 min of point-and-click to one tool call. TCPA gates every import: STOP opt-out language required in every flow's first message, trigger.type must be on the opt-in allowlist, A2P 10DLC brand verified, and quiet-hours respected. Failures REFUSE (no auto-fix) so you see exactly which flow blocked and why.

New: use Merlin from Claude Desktop. The tile-driven setup (brand picker, OAuth, daemons) stays in Merlin; the chat happens wherever you want — Claude Desktop, Codex, Cline, Cursor, any MCP client. On first launch (and after major upgrades), Merlin offers a one-click 'Add to Claude Desktop' that atomically merges itself into claude_desktop_config.json without touching your other MCP servers. Use it as a polished sidecar until the in-app chat catches up.

Security: your API keys never leave Merlin's encrypted vault. Claude Desktop only sees tool inputs and outputs — exactly the same boundary the in-app chat respects. The local IPC endpoint refuses any call outside ping / tools/list / tools/call, so a compromised MCP client can only invoke Merlin tools, never read your tokens.

Active brand syncs automatically. Pick the brand in Merlin's Magic panel; Claude Desktop tools pick it up without you having to type 'brand: pog' on every call. Override is still respected — explicit brand args are never overwritten.
Auth token rotates on every Merlin restart. A stale Claude Desktop tab from a previous session safely refuses old credentials and reconnects on the next call.

Type while Merlin is thinking — your message queues and a 'queued (1)' badge appears next to the spinner so you know it landed. Previously messages typed during a tool call disappeared into a void; the queue existed but the agent never picked them up after the tool finished. Now the next turn drains everything you typed in order.

Merlin announces what it's about to do before any tool that takes >15s or spends real money — including the brand, product, and reference images it's using. Catch a wrong context BEFORE 30+ minutes of generation spend, not after. Vague 'working on it…' is gone.

Image and video generation now requires a real product + reference photos. Merlin will STOP and ask 'which product?' if the brand has multiple products and you didn't name one — instead of silently generating something generic. Closes the live incident where 12 ads got generated without any of the brand's actual references.

Errors stay raw — Merlin no longer guesses at root causes when a tool fails. If it doesn't know what went wrong, it says so and asks what to try next. Banned: invented internals like 'the binary's slug registry doesn't recognize this' when no such structure exists.
Cancel button still escapes hung tool calls, and now correctly clears the queue badge too — clicking Cancel with messages queued shows them cleared so you know the abort worked end-to-end.

Klaviyo template bulk upload — drop a folder of email HTMLs and Merlin uploads them all to Klaviyo with one command, applying token translations ({{UNSUB_URL}} → Klaviyo Django tags) and a configurable naming template. Note: Klaviyo Flows themselves still need to be wired in Klaviyo's UI — flow construction isn't in the public API.

Fixed: any fal.ai model under any namespace now works — pass openai/gpt-image-2/edit, black-forest-labs/flux-pro, or any future-lab/model/v1 and Merlin sends it through unchanged. Previously the binary auto-prepended fal-ai/ to every slash-containing slug, corrupting non-fal-ai/ namespaces into 404s. Live incident: openai/gpt-image-2/edit was unreachable.

New: Cancel button on the chat status row. When a tool call hangs (slow website fetch, third-party API stall), a Cancel button fades in next to the spinner after 30s — one click stops the turn. Previously the only escape was force-quitting Merlin.

Fixed: 'go to my site and check products' / 'rescrape <url>' now reliably routes to the brand-scrape tool (which has hardened 5/15/90s timeouts) instead of the built-in WebFetch (which has no timeout). Live incident: trypog.co rescrape hung the chat for 5+ minutes.

Fixed: when Merlin can't find a Meta access token, the error now says exactly what to do — 'restart Merlin and retry' for brand-scoped tiles, or 'connect Meta via the Magic panel' for global. Previously the same five fatal sites all said 'paste your token and run this again' — which loops the user into the same failure when the actual bug was upstream.

Fixed: pasting a Meta access token via the right-click → 'Use my API key' modal on a brand-scoped tile now works end-to-end. Previously the discover step failed with 'metaAccessToken required' even though the token was correctly saved — the binary spawn passed only the global config, missing the brand's vault placeholder. Now uses the same brand-merged temp config the dashboard uses.

Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
